issues.description: Finish consolidating the canonical Go task, intake, and workflow vocabulary so downstream runtime slices stop inventing parallel compatibility contracts.
issues.title: Unified domain model and intake contract migration

comments.body: Claimed the worker runtime quota-enforcement slice. Applying policy-resolved concurrency and queue-depth limits inside the Go worker scheduler path so runtime decisions enforce tenant/task policy caps instead of only global loop quotas.

What changed:
Applied policy-resolved task quotas inside the Go worker runtime before scheduler admission. The worker now tightens the loop-provided quota snapshot with per-task policy concurrency, queue-depth, and budget caps, and worker tests now cover policy-driven concurrency and backpressure retries.

Validation:
cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/worker ./internal/policy ./internal/scheduler
passed

Commit SHA: a4a0ed426fd14c405eaad1b6c198c1edbf4e5e59
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/140

Claimed the canonical audit-event emission slice. Wiring Go-owned scheduler decisions and control-center takeovers into the observability audit spec so runtime/control actions emit the required P0 audit payload fields instead of leaving the spec registry detached from live events.

What changed:
Wired the canonical audit spec into live Go runtime/control paths. Worker scheduler admissions now emit `execution.scheduler_decision` audit events with risk and approval context, and control-center takeover actions now emit `execution.manual_takeover` audit events with the required handoff fields. Added regression coverage in the worker and API suites to prove both payloads satisfy the audit spec.

Validation:
cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/worker ./internal/api ./internal/observability
passed

Commit SHA: f4df77de2a3dac32a2cf5a88c13a5fb340038d2e
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/140

issues.description: Keep porting governance, execution-contract, audit, and policy semantics into Go so review and approval behavior no longer depends on Python-only code paths.
issues.title: Risk, policy, and approval semantics migration

comments.body: Claimed the workflow orchestration and scheduler loop migration slice for the next Go runtime hardening change.

What changed:
Hardened the Go orchestration loop so it runs one scheduler cycle immediately at startup, returns cleanly when no runtime is configured, and falls back to a safe default poll interval instead of panicking on unset values. Added focused loop tests for immediate startup execution and default interval handling.

Validation:
cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/orchestrator ./cmd/bigclawd
passed

Commit SHA: b45bd88fe6a3f6b31e4e11fc3bc41d33f23e4553
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/140

What changed:
Wired the Go orchestration loop to collect live queue quota metrics before each scheduler cycle. `bigclawd` now feeds the loop a queue-backed quota source so real runtime decisions see current queue depth and active leased work instead of only the static startup limits, and orchestrator tests now cover the live queue-depth/running-count snapshot path.

Validation:
cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/orchestrator ./cmd/bigclawd
passed

Commit SHA: 2bc9c97d3f09c5872ae92c93b98c536ce2e9b279
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/140

What changed:
Extended the live Go loop quota source to report preemptible active capacity as well as queue depth and running-count totals. The queue-backed quota snapshot now uses the scheduler's urgent-priority threshold to count lower-priority leased work that an urgent run can reclaim, so live preemption decisions no longer depend on hand-injected quota fields.

Validation:
cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/orchestrator ./cmd/bigclawd
passed

Commit SHA: 80cecfe888fb1c32a737a590e270c37b3d35b471
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/140

issues.description: Finish moving operator-facing status, control-center, and triage endpoints to Go once the workflow runtime and reporting surfaces are stable.

issues.description: Port the remaining repo board, registry, gateway, linkage, and lineage surfaces into Go without leaking repo-specific concerns into the scheduler runtime.
issues.title: Repo collaboration and lineage surface migration

comments.body:
What changed:
Added a Go-native local tracker closeout path in bigclawctl so local issues can be transitioned and annotated without manual JSON edits. The command now appends completion-style comments with change summary, validation, commit SHA, and PR metadata, and the local issue store preserves those fields on write. Blocker: this workspace origin is a local path (/Users/jxrt/Desktop/symphony-main/BigClaw), so no GitHub PR URL can be refreshed or recorded from the current repo config.

Validation:
cd bigclaw-go && go test ./cmd/bigclawctl ./internal/refill
passed

comments.body:
What changed:
Fixed a Go GitHub sync edge case where a fresh issue branch on origin could remain unpublished if local HEAD matched the remote default-branch SHA. EnsureRepoSync now still performs the first push when the issue branch is missing, and a regression test covers that branch-publication path.

Validation:
cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/githubsync
passed

comments.body: Extended Go workspace validate CLI parity with the legacy Python entrypoint. The command now accepts repeated space-separated values after --issues, includes report_file in JSON output when a report is written, and prints report_file=... ahead of markdown output in non-JSON mode. Added command-level regression tests for repeated issue parsing and report path emission.

Cut the remaining legacy workspace operator entrypoints over to the Go-first wrapper. The Python bootstrap and validation scripts under scripts/ops are now thin subprocess shims into scripts/ops/bigclawctl, the BigClaw-specific shim still injects its repo-url and cache-key defaults, and the bootstrap template now documents the Go wrapper as the canonical required artifact instead of Python src files. Added focused shim-forwarding coverage in tests/test_go_first_workspace_shims.py.

What changed:
Finished the Go-first operator cutover for the workflow/bootstrap/sync toolchain. The Go CLI now matches the legacy workspace-validate issue/report behavior, and every remaining Python compatibility entrypoint under scripts/ops for workspace bootstrap, workspace validate, github-sync, and refill is now a thin subprocess shim into scripts/ops/bigclawctl instead of importing src/bigclaw runtime code. The repo bootstrap template also now treats the Go wrapper as the canonical required artifact.

Validation:
cd bigclaw-go && go test ./cmd/bigclawctl
/Users/jxrt/miniforge3/bin/python3 -m py_compile scripts/ops/symphony_workspace_bootstrap.py scripts/ops/bigclaw_workspace_bootstrap.py scripts/ops/symphony_workspace_validate.py scripts/ops/bigclaw_github_sync.py scripts/ops/bigclaw_refill_queue.py tests/test_go_first_workspace_shims.py
/Users/jxrt/miniforge3/bin/python3 - <<'PY'
import importlib.util
from pathlib import Path
root = Path('/Users/jxrt/code/symphony-workspaces/BIG-GOM-307')
def load(name, relative):
    path = root / relative
    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(name, path)
    module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
    spec.loader.exec_module(module)
    return module
class Completed:
    returncode = 0
def verify(module, argv, expected):
    captured = {}
    def fake_run(command, cwd):
        captured['command'] = command
        captured['cwd'] = cwd
        return Completed()
    module.subprocess.run = fake_run
    code = module.main(argv)
    assert code == 0
    assert captured['command'] == expected
    assert captured['cwd'] == root
verify(load('symphony_bootstrap', 'scripts/ops/symphony_workspace_bootstrap.py'), ['bootstrap', '--workspace', '/tmp/workspace', '--issue', 'BIG-GOM-307'], ['bash', str(root / 'scripts/ops/bigclawctl'), 'workspace', 'bootstrap', '--workspace', '/tmp/workspace', '--issue', 'BIG-GOM-307'])
verify(load('bigclaw_bootstrap', 'scripts/ops/bigclaw_workspace_bootstrap.py'), ['bootstrap', '--workspace', '/tmp/workspace', '--issue', 'BIG-GOM-307'], ['bash', str(root / 'scripts/ops/bigclawctl'), 'workspace', 'bootstrap', '--workspace', '/tmp/workspace', '--issue', 'BIG-GOM-307', '--repo-url', 'git@github.com:OpenAGIs/BigClaw.git', '--cache-key', 'openagis-bigclaw'])
verify(load('bigclaw_bootstrap_override', 'scripts/ops/bigclaw_workspace_bootstrap.py'), ['cleanup', '--workspace', '/tmp/workspace', '--repo-url=https://example.com/repo.git', '--cache-key=custom-cache'], ['bash', str(root / 'scripts/ops/bigclawctl'), 'workspace', 'cleanup', '--workspace', '/tmp/workspace', '--repo-url=https://example.com/repo.git', '--cache-key=custom-cache'])
verify(load('symphony_validate', 'scripts/ops/symphony_workspace_validate.py'), ['--workspace-root', '/tmp/workspaces', '--repo-url', 'git@example.com:repo.git', '--issues', 'BIG-1', 'BIG-2', '--json'], ['bash', str(root / 'scripts/ops/bigclawctl'), 'workspace', 'validate', '--workspace-root', '/tmp/workspaces', '--repo-url', 'git@example.com:repo.git', '--issues', 'BIG-1', 'BIG-2', '--json'])
verify(load('bigclaw_github_sync', 'scripts/ops/bigclaw_github_sync.py'), ['status', '--require-synced', '--json'], ['bash', str(root / 'scripts/ops/bigclawctl'), 'github-sync', 'status', '--require-synced', '--json'])
verify(load('bigclaw_refill_queue', 'scripts/ops/bigclaw_refill_queue.py'), ['--apply', '--watch', '--local-issues', 'local-issues.json'], ['bash', str(root / 'scripts/ops/bigclawctl'), 'refill', '--apply', '--watch', '--local-issues', 'local-issues.json'])
print('all shim forwarding checks passed')
PY
passed

issues.description: Keep hardening the Go-first bootstrap, refill, and GitHub sync toolchain so unattended multi-issue work no longer relies on Python entrypoints or Linear-only operators.
issues.title: Workflow, bootstrap, and GitHub sync toolchain migration

issues.description: Freeze or retire the remaining Python runtime entrypoints only after the Go workflow, observability, and repo collaboration surfaces have matching owners and validation.
issues.title: Python deprecation and Go-only mainline switch
